raw.population: Model group: N: 116 (Chinese) Age: 6.91 (0.5-17) WT: 27.87 (8-85) Validation group: N: 168 Age:8.09 (1-17) WT: 31.82 (6-98)
raw.objective: “to establish a population pharmacokinetics (PPK) model for LTG in Chinese children with epilepsy to formulate an individualized dosage guideline”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, SEX, WT, VPA, OXC, CLNZ, LVT, TPM, IND (CBZ, PHB) Included: WT, VPA, CBZ, PHB on CL; WT on Vd
raw.observations: Unexplained variance of CL reduced from ~65% to ~26%, allowing more accurate individualised dosing in this population
raw.structure: 1 compartment with linear absorption and elimination
raw.parameter_values: theta1=1.01 theta2=0.635 theta3=-0.753 theta4=0.868 theta5=0.633 theta6=16.7 theta7=1 omega1=0.067 sigma1=0.045
raw.validation: Yes (bootstrap-like analysis of 10 subpopulations) / Yes (but not shown)

extraction_target.population: Model group: N: 116 (Chinese) Age: 6.91 (0.5-17) WT: 27.87 (8-85) Validation group: N: 168 Age:8.09 (1-17) WT: 31.82 (6-98)

structural_model.description: 1 compartment with linear absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.population: Model group: N: 116 (Chinese) Age: 6.91 (0.5-17) WT: 27.87 (8-85) Validation group: N: 168 Age:8.09 (1-17) WT: 31.82 (6-98)
study.objective: “to establish a population pharmacokinetics (PPK) model for LTG in Chinese children with epilepsy to formulate an individualized dosage guideline”

covariates.raw: Tested: AGE, SEX, WT, VPA, OXC, CLNZ, LVT, TPM, IND (CBZ, PHB) Included: WT, VPA, CBZ, PHB on CL; WT on Vd

validation.raw: Yes (bootstrap-like analysis of 10 subpopulations) / Yes (but not shown)

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
